programme.title: PBS News Hour
programme.desc: Co-anchors Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett and correspondents offer in-depth analysis of current events.

programme.title: Robert Redford: Star of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: The career of actor, director and producer Robert Redford, including his work promoting independent film through the Sundance Film Festival and Institute and his commitment to environmental and political causes.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: An Andrew Clemens sand bottle circa 1885; a 1933 Goudey baseball card set 239/240; a Joan Miró pencil on paper circa 1965.

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.sub-title: The Times That Try Men's Souls
programme.desc: British General Howe defeats General Washington, forcing the Americans to abandon New York; Washington's army regroups in New Jersey before attacking an outpost at Trenton.

programme.title: Lafayette: The Lost Hero
programme.desc: Marquis de Lafayette left France at the age of 19 and helped fight for America's freedom.

programme.desc: Co-anchors Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett and correspondents offer in-depth analysis of current events.

programme.title: Wild Kratts
programme.desc: The Kratt Bros. discover there is an old wily orca who only eats large sharks; they find this hard to believe, so with the help of Aviva's dolphin decoder, modified to speak orca, the Wild Kratts go in search of the orca who likes sharks.

programme.desc: Lily and her family discover a surprise and also embark on a weather hunting expedition.

programme.desc: Phoebe learns what privacy signs are and how to use them to communicate to neighbors; Jay gets nervous about getting a haircut.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Julius Cohen yellow diamond earrings; a 1928 Gibson Granada banjo; a collection of original comic art dating back to 1937.

programme.desc: Scientists try to figure out how to predict weather events called "rain bombs" that sink ships and down planes.

programme.title: Wilding
programme.desc: Isabella Tree and her husband, Charlie Burrell, strive to breathe new life into the ailing Knepp estate. They reintroduce native ponies, deer, pigs and other wildlife, allowing nature to thrive.

programme.title: California's Golden Parks
programme.desc: The California State Railroad Museum houses restored railroad cars and locomotives that illustrate a unique railroad history.

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: General Howe takes Philadelphia, but the Americans' surprising victory over General Burgoyne at Saratoga encourages France to support the U.S.

programme.title: A More Perfect Union: Inspiring Civic & Civil Conversations Across America
programme.desc: A look at America's founding and how the ideas and values articulated 250 years ago remain relevant to conversations about governance today.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A Van Cleef & Arpels diamong ring; an 1897 San Antonio Folk Art carved cane; a Käthe Kollwitz print and drawing.

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: A fundraising evening at the historic Brokenwood Empire Cinema becomes fatal when a fire breaks out, and after the smoke clears, a businessman is found strangled.

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Murders occur when a British food corporation signs a secret agreement with the Nazis to provide essential food services to the enemy during wartime.

programme.title: PBS News Hour
programme.desc: Co-anchors Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett and correspondents offer in-depth analysis of current events.

programme.title: Life Unearthed With Ariel Waldman
programme.desc: Set in Antarctica's Dry Valleys, Antarctica's glacial and icy ecosystems reveal how frozen landscapes can sustain surprising life forms.

programme.title: Nature
programme.desc: Exploring the unique evolutionary journey of woodpeckers and the role they play in every ecosystem, except Antarctica and Australia.
